2604.06028.title: A Multi-Stage Validation Framework for Trustworthy Large-scale Clinical Information Extraction using Large Language Models
2604.06028.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) show promise for extracting clinically meaningful information from unstructured health records, yet their translation into real-world settings is constrained by the lack of scalable and trustworthy validation approaches. Conventional evaluation methods rely heavily on annotation-intensive reference standards or incomplete structured data, limiting feasibility at population scale. We propose a multi-stage validation framework for LLM-based clinical information extraction that enables rigorous assessment under weak supervision. The framework integrates prompt calibration, rule-based plausibility filtering, semantic grounding assessment, targeted confirmatory evaluation using an independent higher-capacity judge LLM, selective expert review, and external predictive validity analysis to quantify uncertainty and characterize error modes without exhaustive manual annotation. We applied this framework to extraction of substance use disorder (SUD) diagnoses across 11 substance categories from 919,783 clinical notes. Rule-based filtering and semantic grounding removed 14.59% of LLM-positive extractions that were unsupported, irrelevant, or structurally implausible. For high-uncertainty cases, the judge LLM's assessments showed substantial agreement with subject matter expert review (Gwet's AC1=0.80). Using judge-evaluated outputs as references, the primary LLM achieved an F1 score of 0.80 under relaxed matching criteria. LLM-extracted SUD diagnoses also predicted subsequent engagement in SUD specialty care more accurately than structured-data baselines (AUC=0.80). These findings demonstrate that scalable, trustworthy deployment of LLM-based clinical information extraction is feasible without annotation-intensive evaluation.
2604.06028.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）在從非結構化健康記錄中提取臨床有意義的信息方面顯示出潛力，但其在現實世界環境中的應用受到缺乏可擴展和可信的驗證方法的限制。傳統的評估方法在很大程度上依賴於需要大量標註的參考標準或不完整的結構化數據，這限制了在整體人口規模上的可行性。我們提出了一個多階段的驗證框架，用於基於LLM的臨床信息提取，該框架能夠在弱監督下進行嚴格評估。該框架整合了提示校準、基於規則的合理性過濾、語義基準評估、使用獨立的高容量判斷LLM進行的針對性確認評估、選擇性專家審查以及外部預測效度分析，以量化不確定性並表徵錯誤模式，而無需繁瑣的手動標註。我們將這一框架應用於從919,783份臨床筆記中提取11個物質類別的物質使用障礙（SUD）診斷。基於規則的過濾和語義基準去除了14.59%的LLM陽性提取，這些提取是無支持的、無關的或結構上不合理的。對於高不確定性的案例，判斷LLM的評估與主題專家審查顯示出顯著的一致性（Gwet's AC1=0.80）。使用經過判斷評估的輸出作為參考，主要LLM在放寬匹配標準下達到了0.80的F1分數。LLM提取的SUD診斷在預測隨後參與SUD專科護理方面的準確性也超過了結構化數據基準（AUC=0.80）。這些發現表明，基於LLM的臨床信息提取的可擴展、可信的部署在不需要標註密集評估的情況下是可行的。
2604.06028.author: Maria Mahbub et.al.
2604.06028.authors: Maria Mahbub, Gregory M. Dams, Josh Arnold, Caitlin Rizy, Sudarshan Srinivasan, Elliot M. Fielstein, Minu A. Aghevli, Kamonica L. Craig, Elizabeth M. Oliva, Joseph Erdos, Jodie Trafton, Ioana Danciu
